title: Issue 2046 operational effectiveness full lane

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the whole GitHub #2046 operational-effectiveness lane: relevant self-improvement, dogfooding, workflow-obligation, optimization-posture, Planning, Memory, and Verification signals must change ordinary task posture/routing/proof/closeout or report explicit non-applicability.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not close #2046 by fixing only #2045 or #2047; do not add advisory-only prose; do not replace agent judgment with keyword matching; keep broad repo scans behind selectors.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact field names, packet shape, focused fixture coverage, and whether the behavior lands in task_posture_packet, report packets, or closeout packet helpers.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Satisfying the lane would require a general task-management system, automatic issue filing for weak signals, or hiding proof/safety/residue for compactness.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement the operational-effectiveness contract across task posture, dogfooding intake, workflow obligations, optimization effect, and module-effect fixtures.
proof_expectations: Focused CLI fixtures for task posture, session dogfooding intake/status, workflow obligation disposition, optimization effect, and module-effect matrix.
AW implement proof selection for changed paths.
Runtime mirror consistency for mirrored runtime helpers.
completion_criteria: Operational-effectiveness packet classifies each relevant signal as behavior-changing, advisory/non-applicable, or unresolved.
Active improvement pressure changes task posture or explains why it does not.
Session dogfooding observations expose ordinary capture/routing/dismissal states and commands without requiring chat memory or manual issue filing.
Workflow obligations expose disposition states and make diagnostic execution alone insufficient for behavior-changing compliance.
Optimization posture has at least one enforceable ordinary output/proof/selector effect.
Fixtures cover Workspace, Planning, Memory, and Verification signals changing ordinary behavior or being explicitly advisory.
#2046 parent is only claimed complete after this full-lane evidence passes; child #2045/#2047 remain bounded symptoms.

goal: Implement the whole GitHub #2046 operational-effectiveness lane, not only the first slice.
non_goals: Do not close or implement unrelated standalone issues such as #2048/#2049.
Do not make broad repo-friction scans part of ordinary start/implement.
Do not auto-file issues for every weak signal.
Do not hide proof, safety, uncertainty, or residue to satisfy compactness.

intent.outcome: Implement the whole GitHub #2046 operational-effectiveness lane.
intent.constraints: Behavior must be operational, not advisory-only; broad scans stay selector-gated.
intent.latitude: Choose packet fields and fixtures that satisfy the issue acceptance criteria.
intent.escalation: Escalate only if the lane requires auto-issue-filing, a general task manager, or proof/safety hiding.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Implement the #2046 lane in stacked PRs.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Implement the whole #2046 operational-effectiveness lane, not a first slice.

intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Runtime posture/report helpers, mirrored primitives, startup/implement wiring, focused CLI tests, generated command artifacts only if a command-interface change is needed, and #2046 planning records.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Expected upper bound: about 18 files if generated command artifacts are needed; otherwise about 10 files.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest for new fixtures, AW implement proof selection, runtime mirror consistency, make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, tests/test_workspace_cli.py, make typecheck, summary, closeout_trust.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, #2046 issue body, #2046 lane record, runtime task posture/report helpers, and focused CLI tests.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue full #2046 lane implementation; first slice is already closed, but parent lane remains the requested scope.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Whole #2046 lane, including remaining child symptoms and parent acceptance criteria.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Operational effect required; advisory-only outputs do not satisfy the lane; child symptoms alone do not close parent.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact runtime packet shape and fixture placement.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The implementation needs auto-filing every weak signal or a general task manager.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?
